name: Shiu Ng Heung Gai (Oven Roasted Spiced Chicken)
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Chinese wine or dry sherry
recipeInstructions:
This method of preparation is particularly suited for chicken drumsticks, thighs or wings. If it is not possible to buy selected joints, a whole chicken can be used.
Oven temperature: 350 to 375 degrees
Cooking Time: 45 minutes to 1 hour
1. Wash chicken pieces and dry well on paper towels.
2. In a large shallow dish, mix soy sauce, oil and wine. Crush garlic with salt and add to the soy mixture along with ginger and five spice. Mix well.
3. Put chicken joints in the marinade and turn to coat all sides. Cover and marinate for 1 hour or more.
4. Remove chicken from the marinade and place it in a roasting pan in one layer. Spoon about 2 tbsp of the marinade over the chicken.
5. Roast in a moderate oven for 45 minutes to 1 hour, turning the chicken occasionally so it is browned all over. Baste frequently.
6. Wings need about 45 minutes of roasting time, drumsticks from 45-55 minutes and thighs require 1 hour.
7. Thighs should be roasted skin side uppermost.
Variation:
Make a marinade as above and rub it well all over a whole chicken weighing 3 1/2 lb. Spoon some of the marinade into the cavity of the chicken as well. Marinate for 1 hour, then roast as above, turning the chicken first on one side and then on the other so it is browned all over. Baste frequently. Finish cooking the chicken breast upwards. It should take about 1 hour 45 minutes. To serve, carve the chicken as for red-cooked chicken.
